title: lupinus polyphyllus subsp. superbus
intro: Lupinus polyphyllus subsp. superbus, also known as the tall meadow lupine, is a perennial plant species native to the northwestern United States and Canada. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Lupinus polyphyllus subsp. superbus include reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant properties. Herbal preparations of this plant often include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support respiratory health and relieve symptoms of colds and flu.
